messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Problem installing Biostrings - development version
messages.body_plain:
Hello All,

I would like to use the latest version of ShortRead so I am attempting to install development versions of R and Bioconductor. I downloaded the development snapshot of R that was released yesterday, March 31, and it compiled on my system without any problems. Then I tried to install Biobase using biocLite() and it worked until it got to the Biostrings package where it had an error. The package passes the most recent build/check report, but the server (wilson1) is using a snapshot of R from March 1. My first thought was to try using that exact version of R but I was not able to locate it...

I have recreated the error below along with my sessionInfo(). Thanks in advance for any help you can offer!



-------------------------------------------
Karl Dykema
Computational Biologist
Laboratory of Computational Biology
Van Andel Research Institute
333 Bostwick Ave NE
Grand Rapids, MI 49503

messages.body_plain:
Hi Karl,

This problem has been fixed in Biostrings >= 2.11.45 but thanks to your
post I discovered that package updates have not been propagated to our public
software repository for the last couple of days. I'm currently investigating
this and will keep you informed. Thanks!

H.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Sample names on NChannelSet
messages.body_plain:
Hi Martin

my understanding of NChannelSet is that the element matrices of the 
assayData should have the same column names, which are identical with 
the "sampleNames".

Or do you see a convincing use case for different column names?

  Best wishes
	Wolfgang


Martin Morgan wrote:
> Hi Benilton --
> 
> Benilton Carvalho <bcarvalh at jhsph.edu> writes:
> 
>> (apologies in case this is a duplicated message - the first email was
>> returned with an error)
>>
>> NChannelSet accepts the elements of assayData to have different
>> colnames. For example:
>>
>> setClass("myClass", contains="NChannelSet")
>> x <- y <- matrix(0, 5, 5)
>> colnames(x) <- paste("x", 1:5, sep="")
>> colnames(y) <- paste("y", 1:5, sep="")
>> (zz1 <- new("myClass", x=x, y=y))
>> (zz2 <- new("myClass", x=x, y=x))
>>
>> Note that the show method suggests that the sample names for zz1 is
>> x1, ..., x5 (identical for zz2).
>>
>> Also, sampleNames() returns a list:
>>
>>  > sampleNames(zz1)
>> $x
>> [1] "x1" "x2" "x3" "x4" "x5"
>>
>> $y
>> [1] "y1" "y2" "y3" "y4" "y5"
>>
>>  > sampleNames(zz2)
>> $x
>> [1] "x1" "x2" "x3" "x4" "x5"
>>
>> $y
>> [1] "x1" "x2" "x3" "x4" "x5"
>>
>> The fact that NChannelSet accepts different sampleNames also affects
>> subsetting:
>>
>> cols <- c("x1", "x4")
>> (zz1[, cols])  ## Error: subscript out of bounds
>> (zz2[, cols])
>>
>> Is this an intended behavior? I'd expect sample names to be the same
> 
> NChannelSet assayData is meant to have elements that have the same
> dimensions, but that might have different names.
> 
> In terms of subsetting, you're right that subsetting by names (or in
> general subsetting so that you select different columns from different
> assayData elements) is not implemented when the assayData elements
> have different sampleNames (note that zz1[,c(1, 4)] works). I could
> implement a method taking a list to use for subsetting. but I suspect
> that is not really what you're after?
> 
> Martin
> 
>> across elements of AssayData and sampleNames() to return a vector
>> (instead of a list - side-effect of eapply).
>>
>> Thank you,
>>
>> benilton
>>
>> _______________________________________________
>> Bioc-devel at stat.math.ethz.ch mailing list
>> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/bioc-devel
> 


-- 

Best wishes
      Wolfgang

----------------------------------------------------
Wolfgang Huber, EMBL-EBI, http://www.ebi.ac.uk/huber

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] new package for Power and Sample Size Analysis: SSPA
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] new package for Power and Sample Size Analysis: SSPA
messages.body_plain:
Dear list,

A new BioConductor R package which performs Power and Sample Size
Analysis for microarray experiments on the basis of pilot data is added
to the BioConductor Repository called SSPA. 
The package is easy to use, needing only two functions to run the entire
analysis in less than a minute. Methods for visualization and
interpretation of the results are implemented as well. The package can
also be used to perform power and sample size analysis on data such as
array CGH and SNP arrays, as well as other technologies such as deep
sequencing. Reseachers can gain more insight not only for experimental
design but also about the amount of differential expression yielded by
their data.

Best Regards,

Maarten

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] BioC 2.4 Beta 7 April (Tomorrow)
messages.body_plain:
Hello fellow Bioconductors,
As the BioC 2.4 release train moves towards the 21 April release date, 
we hit our first major milestone tomorrow. April 7th is the day when 
Bioconductor packages are expected to be building and checking without 
error. Please check your package's status at

http://bioconductor.org/checkResults/2.4/bioc-LATEST/

In particular, the follow packages are at risk of being dropped from 
BioC 2.4 because they do not meet the no error criterion:

PACKAGES WITH BUILD ERRORS
ArrayTools
beadarraySNP
ITALICS
rMAT
RSNPper
snapCGH
splicegear
stam

PACKAGES WITH CHECK ERRORS
beadarray
flowClust
maSigPro
pamr
pdInfoBuilder

If your package is in one of these lists, you should have been contacted 
by a BioC core team member notifying you of your package's status. If 
you didn't receive an e-mail, please check to see that the maintainer's 
e-mail in your package's DESCRIPTION file is correct. If you are a 
maintainer of a package above, please make your changes NOW to remove 
the errors from your package.

The next major milestone is 14 April when packages are expected to build 
without warnings. If your package is producing warnings, please make 
your changes sooner rather than later to ensure you meet next week's 
deadline.

http://wiki.fhcrc.org/bioc/BioC_2.4_Release_Schedule

Thank you all for participating in the Bioconductor project and let's 
work at having a smooth BioC 2.4 release.


The Biocore Team

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Call for Abstracts for BioC2009 Conference Practicals
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Call for Abstracts for BioC2009 Conference Practicals
messages.body_plain:
The BioC2009 Conference will occur 27-29 July 2009 at the FHCRC in Seattle, and we will be posting conference details at bioconductor.org by week's end.

An important feature of the conference is the afternoon practicals, in which developers and scientists present relevant topics in depth.  We invite contributions of tutorial proposals from the user/developer community.

If you are interested in presenting a tutorial, please send an e-mail to me with your title and abstract. We have a nominal deadline of 30 April for proposals, but extensions can be given upon request.

All applications will be assessed by the BioC2009 program committee to determine acceptance. Presenters will have their conference registration fee waived, but travel and local expenses will not be covered in this package.



Patrick

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Recent changes to geneplotter in BioC 2.4 and R >= 2.9 RC
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Recent changes to geneplotter in BioC 2.4 and R >= 2.9 RC
messages.body_plain:
Hello fellow developers,
Three functions from geneplotter (smoothScatter, panel.smoothScatter, 
and densCols) have recently been promoted to "base" R starting with R 
2.9 RC due to their usage beyond the Bioconductor sphere. As such, these 
three functions were removed from geneplotter on 15 April and the 
following packages have been modified to use smoothScatter, 
panel.smoothScatter, and/or densCols from R 2.9 RC graphics, lattice, 
and grDevices respectively:

affy, affyPLM, affyQCReport, arrayQualityMetrics, beadarray, cellHTS, 
cellHTS2, flowCore, flowViz, GGBase, GGexplorer, GGtools, lumi, maDB, 
oligo, ppiStats, prada, Ringo, spkTools, tilingArray, vsn, XDE, yaqcaffy

Today's builds are in

http://bioconductor.org/checkResults/2.4/bioc-LATEST/

and if you wish to use these new packages you must upgrade to R 2.9 RC 
or R-devel since geneplotter no longer contains the functions mentioned. 
For those interested in seeing package interdependence trimmed down, 
this change reduced the number of packages that Depends/Imports/Suggests 
geneplotter from 23 packages down to 9 packages.

Just as a reminder,  BioC 2.4 release is scheduled for release 21 April 
so check your check-ins using R 2.9 RC to ensure you haven't 
inadvertently introduced a problem.


Cheers,
Patrick

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] BioC 2.4 SVN BRANCH TO BE CREATED, PLEASE REFRAIN FROM SVN SUBMITS FROM 4PM - 6PM PDT
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] BioC 2.4 SVN BRANCH TO BE CREATED, PLEASE REFRAIN FROM SVN SUBMITS FROM 4PM - 6PM PDT
messages.body_plain:
I am happy to announce that we are getting really close to the BioC 2.4 
release. In order to prepare for this release, all developers *must* 
refrain from SVN commits between 4 pm and 6 pm PDT - or until further 
notice. This check-in moratorium is needed because we need to create a 
branch in SVN to hold the BioC 2.4 code line and your check-ins could 
inadvertently cause your package to break become the files may not wind 
up in the SVN repository that you intended it to. Thanks for your help 
on this matter.


Patrick

For those needing to fix bugs in the BioC 2.4 version of your package,

Patrick
